project_overview.project_name: Karachi Water and Sewerage Services Improvement Project (KWSSIP)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve access to safe water services in Karachi and to increase KWSB's financial and operational performance
project_overview.beneficiaries: At least 2,000,000 persons expected to benefit from improved access to safe water services; KWSB utility; residents of Karachi including informal settlements (Katchi Abadis)

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Karachi has experienced security challenges but substantial reduction in violent crime achieved; project areas must exclude zones affected by ongoing Anti-Encroachment Drive (AED)

key_components.description: Capacity building and reform measures including revenue management, customer care, non-revenue water reduction, institutional reforms, human resources development, social sector policy for informal settlements, asset management, and industrial discharge monitoring
key_components.financing: US$7 million (IBRD: US$2.8 million, AIIB: US$2.8 million, GoS: US$1.4 million)

key_components.description: Water network rehabilitation, sewer network rehabilitation, safe water supplies in Katchi Abadis, energy efficiency improvements, sewage suction and jetting trucks, chlorination facilities, metering systems, and district meter areas
key_components.financing: US$77 million (IBRD: US$30.8 million, AIIB: US$30.8 million, GoS: US$15.4 million)

key_components.description: Project management costs, masterplan updates, feasibility studies, tender documents, safeguard documents, energy audits, PPP options studies, groundwater protection assessment, and institutional strengthening
key_components.financing: US$16 million (IBRD: US$6.4 million, AIIB: US$6.4 million, GoS: US$3.2 million)

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Islamic Republic of Pakistan through Province of Sindh via Karachi Water & Sewerage Board (KWSB)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Implementation Unit (PIU) established within KWSB with two departments (one for reforms/studies, one for capital investments); Project Steering Committee providing high-level oversight and multi-stakeholder coordination; Reform Working Group with civil society specialists; coordination with related projects (KNIP, CLICK)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (August 2018); International Competitive Bidding (ICB) for major goods and works; National Competitive Bidding (NCB) for smaller works; Consultancy services using QCBS and CQS methods; Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) prepared
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: M&E integral to project under PIU responsibility; quarterly progress reports to KWSB management; data collection and verification by PIU; beneficiary survey at project closing; 15 intermediate results indicators tracking infrastructure, non-revenue water, metering, grievance redress, informal settlements, gender equity, and energy efficiency

identified_risks.risk_description: Complex political context with multiple actors at all tiers of government; risk of political disagreements affecting project implementation on contentious topics such as target areas, tariff revisions, and employment changes at KWSB
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Collaborative approach between GoS, KMC and KWSB institutionalized through Project Steering Committee and agreed reform roadmap; Reform Working Group with independent sector specialists; public, government and judicial pressure expected to maintain focus on technical solutions; SOP program incentivizes collaboration and performance

identified_risks.risk_description: Deterioration in macro-fiscal situation in Pakistan with currency depreciation, energy tariff hikes, inflationary impacts, and reduced spending power; delayed release of capital and recurrent budgets
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Economic reform program negotiated with IMF; major infrastructure activities scheduled for second half of project life; province to prioritize project expenditures in annual budget and development plans

identified_risks.risk_description: KWSB has not recently implemented significant IFI-financed investments; specific project aspects beyond KWSB's traditional capacity (e.g., service expansion in Katchi Abadis); sustainability risks due to complex political economy of Karachi
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Engagement of consultancy firms for contract management and training; improvement of KWSB administrative systems; consensus-oriented approach to reform agenda; capacity building and technical assistance

identified_risks.risk_description: Sewer network rehabilitation could have significant and widespread adverse environmental impacts requiring strong management; management of raw sewage, contaminated spoil material, solid waste, poor quality water, and air quality; potential environmental legacy of selected sewer networks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental Management Framework (EMF) prepared with screening criteria; full Environmental and Social Impact Assessment (ESIA) and Environmental and Social Management Plan (ESMP) required for all rehabilitation and new works; Environmental Audit and Management Action Plan for ongoing sewer rehabilitation; KWSB environmental capacity strengthening; dedicated Environment Safeguards Specialist in PIU

identified_risks.risk_description: Risks associated with ongoing judiciary-led Anti-Encroachment Drive (AED) which is not consistent with Bank policies on Involuntary Resettlement (OP 4.12); lack of documentation on AED-affected areas may result in unknowingly selecting subproject sites; difficulty finding eligible subproject sites due to extent of AED coverage; limited involuntary economic displacement and temporary negative livelihood impacts during construction
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Elaborate screening criteria built into project design and included in legal agreements; AED-affected areas excluded from project financing; Project Risk Reducing Procedure (PRRP) with robust screening process to exclude non-compliant sites; third-party monitoring to confirm sites not subjected to AED; multi-level Grievance Redress Mechanism; extensive capacity building for KWSB on safeguards management; communication campaign to raise awareness; coordination with CLICK project for city-wide approach to resettlement issues

identified_risks.risk_description: KWSB lacks detailed knowledge of World Bank requirements; need to enhance FM capacity; need to strengthen internal audit function; need for documenting Standard Operating Procedures
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: FM Specialist to be hired within 3 months of effectiveness; Standard Operating Procedures for FM management to be developed; plan to strengthen internal audit function; quarterly Interim Unaudited Financial Reports; annual external audits by Auditor General; regular FM supervision missions

identified_risks.risk_description: Progressive development of sector strategies and policies critical for success; possible resistance to change in sector policies (e.g., KWSB Act amendments, Masterplan, HR policy)
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Integrative approach with high-level steering committee and reform working group with broad stakeholder representation including civil society; consensus-finding process for proposed policy changes

identified_risks.risk_description: Various stakeholders with often difficult to reconcile interests; formal and informal organizations, private companies, NGOs, religious institutions, local government structures vying for influence; selection of informal settlements could lead to controversy and opposition from local interest groups (e.g., water vendors); implementation delays
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Sustained professional communications and outreach program under Component 1; work with local councilors and residents through revitalized Katchi Abadi Cell; establishment of small local offices in selected Katchi Abadis; funding for NGOs and CBOs working in Katchi Abadis; awareness raising and sensitization to distinguish between AED and WB subprojects; supplementation by Bank's Karachi Platform communication and outreach

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Multi-level GRM including: project-specific GRM for subprojects; strengthened KWSB existing complaints management system (phone, email, WhatsApp, SMS, Pakistan Citizen Portal); new customer service centers with women's desks; gender-sensitive GRM with protocol for handling GBV complaints; integration of KWSB-wide GRMs with KWSSIP-specific GRM; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) and Inspection Panel available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Substantial gender gaps identified: women often unable to reach public service providers; limited access due to poorly-planned infrastructure and socio-cultural norms; threats to public safety; inadequate facilities at KWSB (168 female staff among 11,700 total; 1.4% at regular employee level, 0.9% at officer level). Project will: increase women employees at all levels with focus on technical/managerial hires; conduct rapid assessment of gender-sensitive workplace infrastructure gaps; provide HR trainings with gender modules; establish women's desks in customer centers staffed by women; ensure gender-disaggregated data collection; develop Gender Action Plan; target 50% women participation in PIU; target 20% female recruitment at Grade 16 and below, 30% at Grade 17 and above; address barriers to women accessing services and grievance redress
vulnerable_groups: Residents of informal settlements (Katchi Abadis) - nearly half of Karachi's residents
Street vendors, hawkers, and informal water operators affected by Anti-Encroachment Drive
Persons without land title and squatters

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed with conflict sensitivity considerations: avoids areas with substantial social risks through PRRP screening; excludes areas affected by ongoing Anti-Encroachment Drive to avoid exacerbating tensions; includes extensive stakeholder engagement and communication strategy to build consensus and manage expectations; focuses on service delivery improvements to strengthen citizen-state confidence; emphasizes inclusive development and representation of vulnerable groups; includes grievance redress mechanisms to address complaints; coordinates with CLICK project for city-wide approach to managing resettlement and rehabilitation issues; recognizes need to rebuild trust with KWSB as service provider
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Karachi faces multiple fragility factors: livability and competitiveness declined (ranks 137 of 140 cities globally); nearly half residents live in informal settlements; severe infrastructure gaps (over $9 billion identified); inadequate water supply (only half of demand met) and sanitation services (6 million lack access); poor drainage and flooding vulnerability; weak institutional coordination among over a dozen agencies; informal land management enabling large-scale encroachment; complex political economy with multiple actors at different government tiers; gender disparities and limited female participation; economic constraints with poverty and inequality; climate vulnerability to floods, droughts, and saltwater intrusion; recent security challenges (though substantial reduction in violent crime achieved)
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Karachi has experienced security challenges but substantial reduction in violent crime achieved through concerted government efforts; project areas must exclude zones affected by ongoing judiciary-led Anti-Encroachment Drive to avoid security risks and ensure compliance with Bank policies; project includes security considerations in design of customer service centers and field operations; awareness campaigns to distinguish WB project from AED to avoid confusion and potential security issues; coordination with local authorities and councilors for community engagement
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Limited involuntary economic displacement and temporary negative livelihood impacts anticipated during construction in densely populated urban areas; rehabilitation works may cause limited involuntary resettlement of temporary markets, vendors, and street hawkers; OP 4.12 (Involuntary Resettlement) triggered; Social Management Framework (SMF) and Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared; site-specific Resettlement Action Plans (RAPs) to be prepared for subprojects with involuntary resettlement; Project Risk Reducing Procedure (PRRP) excludes areas already impacted by Anti-Encroachment Drive; compensation and rehabilitation provisions required for affected persons including those without land title, squatters, vendors, hawkers; third-party monitoring to verify compliance; grievance redress mechanisms in place; coordination with CLICK project for city-wide resettlement and rehabilitation approach
